linkedin.text:
The most valuable thing that happened at the last major incentive trip I produced wasn't on the agenda.

It was a conversation between a VP of Sales and a VP of Product at 11pm on a rooftop in Lisbon — the kind of honest, unguarded exchange that months of calendar-managed one-on-ones had never produced.

That's what changes when you move people out of their daily environment and into a genuinely extraordinary one.

We've delivered 1,500+ events across 130+ destinations for 25,000+ participants. The brief from every client is different. The outcome they all describe afterward is the same: something shifted in the team that the office could not have created.

If you're scoping a conference, product launch, gala, or incentive trip for your organisation, I'm happy to share what we've seen work — and what quietly fails — across different sectors and team sizes. What's on your planning radar right now?

instagram.text:
130+ destinations. One rule: make it unforgettable. 🌍

Tokyo to Patagonia. Lisbon to Cape Town. In 16 years of corporate event production, we've learned that the real breakthroughs — the conversations that shift strategy, the relationships that seal partnerships — happen off the main stage.

They happen at dinner. On a boat. In a souk. During the unexpected.

1,500+ events delivered.
25,000+ participants moved.
16 years of turning business trips into defining moments.

Save this if you're planning a corporate retreat or incentive trip and want a team who's already been there. 📍

→ DM us your dream destination and we'll tell you exactly what we'd build around it.

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateRetreat #EventProduction #MICE #B2BEvents #TeamBuilding #GlobalEvents #CorporateTravel #EventPlanning #UproductionEvents

facebook.text:
Tokyo. Lisbon. Patagonia. Marrakech. Cape Town.

In 16 years, we've taken corporate teams to 130+ destinations and watched something consistent happen: the moment people stop being colleagues and start being a team is almost never in a conference room.

It happens on a shared rooftop. On a coastal trail. At a table with food nobody ordered but everyone devoured.

1,500+ events. 25,000+ participants. And the same brief every time: make this feel nothing like work — but deliver everything the business needs.

Where has your team had its best 'off-site moment'? Tell us in the comments. 🌍

image_prompt: A candid wide-angle photograph of a group of forty corporate attendees in smart-casual attire exploring a narrow, lantern-lit alley in a historic Moroccan medina at golden hour, carrying small shopping bags and laughing together, with ornate tiled walls and vibrant spice stalls filling the background.
